entries.hypothesis: Add concern-ranking synthesis step before questioning — rank top 3 concerns before first question to lead interrogation with highest-impact issues
entries.notes: 3-3 on positive cases, 4 draws on negatives. Step 4 concern-ranking instruction did not reliably change first-question behavior — models still opened with context-gathering questions. Champion was sharper on challenge-forward cases (poke-holes, challenge-my-thinking, uncertain-feature). eval_quality below 0.6 floor.

entries.hypothesis: Prohibit context-gathering openers — step 4 rule: open with challenge not clarification
entries.notes: 3-2 on positive cases (up from 3-3 in iter 1), but eval_quality still below 0.6 floor. Step 4 worked on poke-holes and is-this-good-idea (more forceful challenge) but not on uncertain-feature or challenge-my-thinking (model rationalized context as prerequisite to challenge). Improvement was directional but insufficient.

entries.hypothesis: Assume worst-case and challenge directly — add Core Behavior paragraph: when context thin, assume most common failure mode and challenge it directly instead of warm-up questions
entries.notes: 1 candidate win (is-this-good-idea), 2 champion wins (poke-holes, what-am-i-missing), 7 draws. Thin-context paragraph did not reliably change first-move behavior — model still opened with context-gathering questions on 4/6 positive cases. Only fired correctly on is-this-good-idea where the rewrite premise was explicit enough to assume the failure mode (rewrite-vs-fix not justified). The paragraph fights deeply-ingrained model behavior: when input is genuinely ambiguous (microservices for what system?), model judges that assumptions would be too broad to challenge. eval_quality below 0.6 floor.

entries.hypothesis: Add concrete example of challenge-forward opener vs context-gathering — show ideal first response pattern using poke-holes scenario with ACID failure mode
entries.notes: 6-0 on positive cases, 4 draws on negatives. Adding one concrete example (poke-holes scenario showing challenge-forward vs context-gathering) reliably changed first-move behavior across all 6 positive cases. Model generalizes the example pattern to other topics (auth rewrite, serverless, microservices, real-time collab). Example gives a template the model follows where explicit rules failed in iterations 1-3. eval_quality 0.8, well above 0.6 floor.

entries.hypothesis: Add second example for uncertain/validation requests — challenge premise not context
entries.notes: Candidate only won the exact uncertain-feature case. is-this-good-idea and the remaining positive cases were draws, as were all four negative trigger cases. That leaves eval_quality at 0.55 (1 win, 9 draws), below the 0.6 floor, so the added example was too narrow to beat the current champion.

entries.hypothesis: Add domain-specific failure modes section — explicit instruction to cite 2-3 common real-world failure modes for the specific technology/decision in the opening challenge
entries.notes: Won 4-1 on positive cases (explicit-interrogate, poke-holes, what-am-i-missing, uncertain-feature). Lost challenge-my-thinking, drew is-this-good-idea. All 4 negatives correct draws. However, eval_quality 0.65 < champion 0.8 due to promotion check comparing absolute metrics not just head-to-head. The domain-specific failure modes list was mostly ignored by the model — both candidate and champion opened with context-gathering on most cases. The instruction did not reliably change first-move behavior. Need a fundamentally different approach to force challenge-forward openers.

entries.hypothesis: Restructure skill — move challenge-forward example to immediately after Core Behavior, before research instructions, to increase salience
entries.notes: Champion won 4-1 on positive cases. Restructuring made things WORSE — the candidate produced thinner context-gathering openers that judges rated lower than champion. Multiple executors noted the globally-installed skill shadows the modified SKILL.md in temp eval directories. The structural change to instruction ordering had no detectable positive effect on challenge-forward behavior. eval_quality 0.35, far below 0.6 floor.
